paper_title: Comparative and Evolutionary Analysis of Grass Pollen Allergens Using Brachypodium distachyon as a Model System

methods.method_name: Sequence Retrieval and Identification of Conserved Domains
methods.description: This method involves retrieving protein sequences of pollen allergens from ryegrass and Timothy grass, using them as seed sequences to search against the B. distachyon proteome database, and identifying conserved domains in these sequences.

steps.description: Protein sequences from ryegrass and Timothy grass were selected as representative members and were later used as seed sequences to search against B. distachyon proteome database.
steps.searchable_summary: retrieve protein sequences from ryegrass and Timothy grass
outputs: seed sequences for B. distachyon search

steps.description: All the protein sequences were retrieved from NCBI.
Conserved domains were identified in each pollen allergen group using SDAP and further verified using SMART and Pfam.
steps.searchable_summary: identify and verify conserved domains using SDAP, SMART, and Pfam
